skill.name: lab-prep-calculations
skill.description: Perform pre-experiment calculations and generate step-by-step preparation protocols for dilutions, solution preparation, dissolution, and % concentrations when planning formulations and lab prep workflows.
skill.summary: Automate lab dilution, solution preparation, and concentration calculations with step-by-step protocol generation.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 34 potential issues but all are false positives. The C2 keyword detections refer to the chemistry dilution formula C1V1=C2V2 (Concentration 2), not Command and Control terminology. Weak cryptographic algorithm detections are chemistry notation (molarity M, molecular weight MW, percentage signs). Ruby/shell backtick detections are markdown inline code formatting. Hardcoded URL is the skill's source repository citation. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, network calls, or file system access.

low_findings.title: C2 keyword misidentified as C2 terminology
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'C2' as Command and Control keywords across 10 locations. However, C2 in C1V1=C2V2 is the standard chemistry dilution formula where C2 means final target concentration. Verified in SKILL.md lines 19, 35, 40, 42, 72.

low_findings.title: Chemistry notation misidentified as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged chemistry notation (molarity M, molecular weight MW, percentage signs) as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are standard chemistry formulas for dilution and solution preparation, not cryptographic functions.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as Ruby shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick-enclosed text as Ruby/shell execution. All instances are markdown inline code formatting for file references (e.g., `references/guide.md`, `lab_prep_calculations_result.md`), not command execution.

low_findings.title: Lab checklist descriptions misidentified as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lab validation steps (e.g., V1 <= V2 checks, equipment range checks) as system reconnaissance. These are standard chemistry protocol validation rules in references/guide.md.

low_findings.title: Source repository URL flagged as network beacon
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the GitHub source URL as a hardcoded/network endpoint. This is the skill's source attribution link to the aipoch/medical-research-skills repository, not a malicious data exfiltration target.

content.user_title: Calculate Lab Dilutions and Solution Preparations
content.value_statement: Scientists waste time calculating dilutions and solution concentrations manually. This skill automates C1V1=C2V2 calculations, serial dilutions, and solid preparation formulas with step-by-step protocols.
actual_capabilities: Calculate stock-to-working solution dilutions using C1V1 = C2V2 formula
Generate multi-step serial dilution protocols for large dilution factors
Compute solid reagent mass requirements with molecular weight and purity corrections
Convert between percentage concentration types (w/v, v/v, w/w)
Normalize units across molarity, volume, and mass scales automatically
Output complete lab protocols with equipment lists and safety warnings
limitations: Does not execute physical lab work or control laboratory equipment
Cannot verify actual reagent purity or stock concentration values
Does not model complex solution chemistry like pH buffers or solubility limits
Assumes ideal mixing with no volume contraction or temperature effects

use_cases.title: Working Solution from Stock
use_cases.description: Calculate exact stock and solvent volumes when preparing a working concentration from a concentrated stock solution.

use_cases.title: Solid Reagent Dissolution
use_cases.description: Compute the precise mass of solid chemical needed to prepare a target volume and concentration, including purity corrections.

use_cases.title: Serial Dilution Design
use_cases.description: Structure multi-step serial dilutions when a single dilution step exceeds practical pipetting limits or equipment range.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Stock Dilution
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to prepare [volume] of [concentration] [solute] from a [concentration] stock solution. Calculate the required stock volume and solvent volume, and give me a step-by-step protocol.
prompt_templates.scenario: Diluting a stock solution to a working concentration

prompt_templates.title: Solid to Solution Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to prepare [volume] of [concentration] [solute] from solid powder. The molecular weight is [MW], purity is [purity]. Calculate the mass needed and provide a dissolution protocol.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a solution from a solid chemical

prompt_templates.title: Serial Dilution Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a serial dilution to go from [starting concentration] to [target concentration]. Maximum dilution factor per step is [factor]. Show each intermediate concentration and transfer volume.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a dilution series for an assay standard curve

prompt_templates.title: Percentage Solution Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert [percentage]% [w/v or v/v] solution to molar concentration for [solute] with molecular weight [MW]. Include density conversion if needed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting between percentage and molar concentration units

output_examples.input: Prepare 50 mL of 10 mM NaCl from 1.0 M stock
output: 1. Label a container: 10 mM NaCl, 50 mL.
2. Pipette 0.50 mL of 1.0 M NaCl stock.

output_examples.input: Calculate mass of NaCl needed for 250 mL of 0.9% w/v saline
output: 2. Transfer to a 250 mL volumetric flask.
3. Add approximately 200 mL water and dissolve.
Equipment: Balance, spatula, 250 mL volumetric flask

best_practices: Always specify units explicitly (mM, M, mL, L) to prevent unit conversion errors.
Confirm stock concentration values and reagent purity before performing calculations.
Validate that calculated pipetting volumes are within your equipment range.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without specifying units for concentrations and volumes.
Requesting calculations without considering solubility limits or minimum weighable mass.
Applying dilution formulas when chemical reactions or buffer equilibria affect concentration.

faq.question: What types of calculations can this skill perform?
faq.answer: It handles dilutions using C1V1 = C2V2, solid preparation with molecular weight and purity correction, serial dilutions, and percentage concentration conversions.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any code or scripts?
faq.answer: No. This is a documentation-only skill that guides calculation and protocol generation. No code, scripts, or external commands are executed.

faq.question: Can this skill control lab equipment?
faq.answer: No. It provides calculated volumes and step-by-step instructions, but it cannot control pipettes, balances, or any laboratory hardware.

faq.question: How accurate are the calculations?
faq.answer: Calculations follow standard formulas with unit normalization. Accuracy depends on correct input values for concentration, volume, and molecular weight.

faq.question: Does this skill work with any solute or chemical?
faq.answer: It works with any chemical where the molecular weight and target concentration are known. For percentage solutions, density is needed for w/w conversions.

faq.question: Can I use this for serial dilutions?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can structure multi-step serial dilutions when a single dilution factor exceeds practical pipetting limits.
